_meta.scenario: Vanilla docker compose (no Lando) — path-signal fixture
_meta.source: Captured live by this QA run (feature 005) against a real, disposable `docker compose` project — not synthesized. Reproduction: `compose.yaml` with `name: trqa-vanilla-path`, one `alpine:3` service, `docker compose up -d` from a scratch tmpdir, then `docker inspect tr-qa-vanilla-path-app --format '{{json .Config.Labels}}'`. Container removed immediately after capture (`docker compose down`); no lasting container exists on the host.
_meta.note: com.docker.compose.project.working_dir was captured as the *unresolved* tmpdir path (macOS `$TMPDIR`, e.g. `/var/folders/.../T/tr-qa-vanilla-compose.XXXX`), confirming live that vanilla Compose does NOT itself realpath-resolve a symlinked working directory before stamping the label — this is exactly the case `safeRealpath()`/`resolvedCwd` in src/ingest/docker.ts exists to handle. The path value below is the literal captured tmpdir from this run's capture session; it is used here only as label shape/content, not compared against any specific cwd by these fixture tests (the matcher tests below use fresh scratch dirs per test case for exact/ancestor/sibling scenarios).
